original_hypothesis.title: Healthy Isolate Loans Can Be Forced Into Auction
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ValidateLogic.validateIsolateAuctionLoan does not verify that the loan's health factor is below the liquidation threshold before allowing an auction to start. This could allow attackers to force healthy loans into auction.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Auctions are punitive measures for undercollateralized loans. If the health factor is not checked when transitioning a loan from ACTIVE to AUCTION, attackers can grief users by forcing their healthy positions into auction, potentially extracting bid fines or acquiring NFTs at a discount.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Missing health factor check in validateIsolateAuctionLoan

preconditions: The executeIsolateAuction function (not fully provided but relies on ValidateLogic) does not independently check the health factor

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: State transition to a punitive phase without verifying the punitive condition
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time a user has an active isolate loan

operational_questions: Does executeIsolateAuction check the health factor independently?
Can a user lose their NFT or pay a fine if their healthy loan is auctioned?
validation_requirements: Review IsolateLiquidation.sol (Phase D) to confirm if HF is checked
Attempt to call isolateAuction on a loan with HF > 1

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify a healthy isolate loan with valuable NFT collateral
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: The loan enters the AUCTION state despite being healthy
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Attacker waits for auction duration and liquidates the NFT, or forces the owner to redeem and pay a bid fine

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Users lose trust in the protocol as healthy positions are liquidated
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires contract upgrade to add HF check

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Auctions starting for loans with HF > 1
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Specific addresses initiating auctions on healthy loans

validation_result.mechanism: missing health factor check on auction initiation
validation_result.impact_summary: validateIsolateAuctionLoan allows transitioning an isolate loan from ACTIVE to AUCTION without verifying the loan's health factor is below the liquidation threshold, enabling any user to force healthy loans into auction.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The function validateIsolateAuctionLoan at ValidateLogic.sol:469-482 is called to validate each loan when an isolate auction is initiated. When loanData.loanStatus == Constants.LOAN_STATUS_ACTIVE (first bid, transitioning from ACTIVE to AUCTION), the function performs only basic checks: group validity, caller != owner, loan status, and asset match. It does NOT check whether the loan's health factor is below the liquidation threshold. Critically, the function doesn't even receive the parameters necessary to compute a health factor (no debtAssetData, no nftAssetData, no priceOracle). This stands in stark contrast to validateIsolateBorrowLoan (lines ~392-430), which receives all these parameters and explicitly checks `nftLoanResult.healthFactor >= Constants.HEALTH_FACTOR_LIQUIDATION_THRESHOLD`. The pattern of the codebase centralizes validation in ValidateLogic, and the validate function for auction is clearly missing this critical check.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is directly visible in the function signature and body. validateIsolateAuctionLoan does not receive debtAssetData, nftAssetData, or priceOracle - the three parameters needed to compute the health factor via GenericLogic.calculateNftLoanData. The function body contains no health factor computation or check. Compare with validateIsolateBorrowLoan which calls GenericLogic.calculateNftLoanData and requires healthFactor >= HEALTH_FACTOR_LIQUIDATION_THRESHOLD.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When loanData.loanStatus == LOAN_STATUS_ACTIVE, this function allows the loan to transition to AUCTION state without verifying the loan is actually unhealthy (health factor < 1). The function doesn't receive debtAssetData, nftAssetData, or priceOracle parameters needed to compute health factor.

execution_path_analysis.entry_point: IsolateLiquidation.executeIsolateAuction (not in scope, but calls ValidateLogic)
calling_sequence: 1. Attacker calls isolateAuction via IsolateLiquidation proxy for a healthy loan
2. IsolateLiquidation calls ValidateLogic.validateIsolateAuctionBasic (lines 440-460) - basic pool/asset/param checks pass
3. IsolateLiquidation calls ValidateLogic.validateIsolateAuctionLoan (lines 469-482) - checks loan status is ACTIVE, caller != owner, asset matches - ALL PASS for healthy loan
4. No health factor check occurs anywhere in the validation path
5. Loan transitions from ACTIVE to AUCTION state despite being healthy
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The validateIsolateAuctionLoan function (ValidateLogic.sol:469-482) is the per-loan validation for auction initiation. When called with a loan in ACTIVE status, it only checks: (1) group is valid via validateGroupBasic, (2) caller is not the token owner, (3) loan status is ACTIVE or AUCTION, (4) reserve asset matches. No health factor is computed or checked. The function signature doesn't even include the parameters needed (debtAssetData, nftAssetData, priceOracle) to perform such a check. Compare with validateIsolateBorrowLoan (ValidateLogic.sol:~392-430) which takes these parameters and calls GenericLogic.calculateNftLoanData to verify health factor.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment configuration affects this vulnerability. The defect is in the validation logic itself.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: An attacker identifies a healthy isolate loan (health factor > 1) with valuable NFT collateral. The attacker calls executeIsolateAuction with the NFT token ID and a bid amount. The validation in validateIsolateAuctionBasic passes (pool/asset basic checks). The validation in validateIsolateAuctionLoan passes (loan status is ACTIVE, caller != owner, asset matches). No health factor check prevents the auction. The loan enters AUCTION state. The NFT owner must now either redeem (paying bid fine) or risk losing their NFT through liquidation after auction duration expires. The attacker can grief any isolate loan holder or potentially acquire NFTs at below-market prices.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. Any isolate loan in ACTIVE status can be forced into auction regardless of its health factor. This enables griefing attacks against healthy positions, forcing borrowers to pay bid fines to redeem their NFTs, and potentially allows attackers to acquire NFT collateral at a discount through the auction mechanism. The vulnerability affects all isolate margin lending positions in the protocol.
recommended_mitigations: Add debtAssetData, nftAssetData, and priceOracle parameters to validateIsolateAuctionLoan
When loanData.loanStatus == LOAN_STATUS_ACTIVE (first bid), call GenericLogic.calculateNftLoanData and require healthFactor < HEALTH_FACTOR_LIQUIDATION_THRESHOLD
Skip the health factor check when loanData.loanStatus == LOAN_STATUS_AUCTION (subsequent bids) since the loan is already in auction
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy full protocol with pool, ERC20 debt asset, and ERC721 NFT asset configured with auction params. 2. User deposits NFT in isolate mode and borrows a small amount (health factor >> 1). 3. Attacker calls isolateAuction on the loan with a bid amount. 4. Verify the loan transitions to AUCTION state despite having healthy HF. 5. Verify the NFT owner must pay bid fine to redeem.
uncertainties: IsolateLiquidation.sol (the calling module) is not in scope - it's possible (but unlikely given the ValidateLogic pattern) that executeIsolateAuction has an independent health factor check before calling validateIsolateAuctionLoan
The exact state changes after validateIsolateAuctionLoan passes are not visible since IsolateLiquidation.sol is not provided
additional_testing_needed: Review IsolateLiquidation.sol source code to confirm no independent health factor check exists in executeIsolateAuction
Test calling isolateAuction on a loan with health factor significantly above 1.0
Verify the complete auction flow and whether the borrower can be forced to pay bid fines on healthy positions
